summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing addressing multiple cases including variance requests, an appeal denial, a conditional use request for ground-mounted solar panels, and a withdrawal of an application. The board appointed a new secretary, approved and denied several resolutions, and scheduled a special meeting for June 23, 2022.
decisions: Appointed Mr. Curry as new BZA secretary
Approved variance request for CDDG Goshen LLC regarding sidewalk requirements at 1910 State Route 28 (BZA 2022-001)
Approved resolution denying variance request for accessory structure/privacy fence at 1910 State Route 28 (BZA 2022-002)
Approved variance for Victor Capitola/Capital Custom Homes for non-conforming principle structure at 6981 Greenstone Trace (BZA 2022-003)
Accepted withdrawal of appeal application by Sierra Lewis (BZA 2022-004)
Approved resolution denying appeal of planning and zoning director's decision for Scott Humphreys and Amy Campbell at 2439 Woodville Pike (BZA 2022-005)
Approved variance to construct principle structure behind existing accessory structures for Scott Humphreys and Amy Campbell (BZA 2022-006)
Approved variance reducing rear setback from 35 feet to 16 feet for Dave and Christina Tutor at 6866 Clubside Drive for covered patio (BZA 2022-007)
Approved conditional use for 1200 sq ft ground-mounted solar panels for Rodney and Victoria Siebel at 6790 Gaines Road (BZA 2022-008)
Scheduled special BZA meeting for June 23, 2022 at 6:30 PM
action_items: Mr. Curry to serve as new BZA secretary
Staff to notify applicants of board decisions in accordance with legal authority
Schedule and notify members of special BZA meeting on June 23, 2022
key_amounts: 35-foot rear setback reduced to 16 feet for Tutor covered patio variance (BZA 2022-007)
200 square feet maximum lot area for freestanding solar panels per current code vs. 1078 square feet (two arrays: 11x56 and 11x42) requested for solar panel conditional use (BZA 2022-008)
20-foot wide private drainage easement on Tutor property
15-foot utility easement and 15x15 fire hydrant easement noted on Clubside Drive property
notable_topics: Variance for sidewalk requirements in TC district (CDDG Goshen LLC)
Denial of privacy fence variance in front yard
Non-conforming principle structure classification at Greenstone Trace
Denial of appeal of planning and zoning director's decision (Humphreys/Campbell)
Variance for new principle structure behind non-conforming agricultural accessory structures
Rear setback variance for covered patio at Clubside Drive near golf course pond
Conditional use approval for ground-mounted solar panels exceeding 200 sq ft code limit
Discussion of need to update zoning code for solar panel installations
Scheduling of special June meeting due to Juneteenth federal holiday
attendees: Sonny Namenor / Robert (Solar Freedom contractor representative)
notes: This is a Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) meeting, not a Board of Trustees meeting. Multiple members abstained from votes on cases they were not present for at the original hearing. The board discussed the need to update the township zoning resolution to better accommodate solar panel installations as current code limits freestanding solar panels to 200 square feet. The May BZA meeting was canceled. The board adopted Juneteenth as a holiday per federal mandate, necessitating rescheduling.
title: Goshen Twp. BZA Mtg 4/18/22
